sourceMovie.title: How to Make a Killing
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: How to Make a Killing works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 3000 Miles to Graceland
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If How to Make a Killing clicked for you, 3000 Miles to Graceland (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and thriller, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after How to Make a Killing?
faq.answer: Try 3000 Miles to Graceland, Sukiyaki Western Django, John Wick: Chapter 2—each shares DNA with How to Make a Killing while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is How to Make a Killing?
faq.answer: How to Make a Killing (2026) is best described as Comedy, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is How to Make a Killing worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
